comments.body:
Hello Christian,

This is a very common scenario — WooCommerce treats Puerto Rico as a separate country (country code "PR"), so it won't automatically fall under your domestic US shipping zone even though USPS ships there at domestic rates.

Before we dig in, could you let us know what specifically is happening? For example:
- Are USPS Priority Mail rates not showing at checkout for Puerto Rico orders?
- Are rates showing but you're hitting an error when generating a label?


In the meantime, the most likely fix is to check your shipping zone setup:


1. Go to WooCommerce > Settings > Shipping > Shipping Zones.
2. Check whether Puerto Rico (PR) is included in any of your existing zones. Since WooCommerce lists it as a separate country, it will not be covered by a generic "United States" zone unless you explicitly add it.
3. If there's no zone covering PR, either add Puerto Rico to your existing US domestic zone or create a new zone for it.
4. Make sure the WooCommerce Shipping Services (WSS) shipping method is enabled for that zone, and that USPS Priority Mail is selected as an active service within the WSS app for that zone.


Please share what you're seeing (a screenshot or the specific error message would be very helpful) and we'll get this resolved for you.

Thanks and regards,
Sibi
Team PluginHive
comments.html_body:
Hello Christian,
 
This is a very common scenario — WooCommerce treats Puerto Rico as a separate country (country code "PR"), so it won't automatically fall under your domestic US shipping zone even though USPS ships there at domestic rates.
 
Before we dig in, could you let us know what specifically is happening? For example:
  • Are USPS Priority Mail rates not showing at checkout for Puerto Rico orders?
  • Are rates showing but you're hitting an error when generating a label?
 
In the meantime, the most likely fix is to check your shipping zone setup:
 
  1. Go to WooCommerce > Settings > Shipping > Shipping Zones.
  2. Check whether Puerto Rico (PR) is included in any of your existing zones. Since WooCommerce lists it as a separate country, it will not be covered by a generic "United States" zone unless you explicitly add it.
  3. If there's no zone covering PR, either add Puerto Rico to your existing US domestic zone or create a new zone for it.
  4. Make sure the WooCommerce Shipping Services (WSS) shipping method is enabled for that zone, and that USPS Priority Mail is selected as an active service within the WSS app for that zone.
 
Please share what you're seeing (a screenshot or the specific error message would be very helpful) and we'll get this resolved for you.
 
Thanks and regards,
Sibi
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Need assistance from the L3 team.

Case: USPS Rates Failure for Puerto Rico

Details: A WSS customer on the $29 Popular plan has reported that USPS rates are failing for Puerto Rico. The rate request returns a "Bad Request" error.

From the initial investigation, it appears to be related to the country code being passed as "PR". We handled a similar issue previously for a Stamps.com account under ticket ID: https://pluginhive.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/393254

In this case, the customer is using a USPS Ship account.

Please refer to the following rate log for further investigation:
Rate Log ID: b27de6ec-9b7b-4346-ab04-0a6879f59fb3
I have also attached the request and response ZIP files for your review.

Could you please check and provide your input

Store Details:
Store URL: https://shop.enfigcarstereo.com
Account ID: 9b82d09f-dacf-4e47-8b2d-e11700a134bb

comments.body:
Hello Christian,

Thank you for your patience.

Our team has checked your case further and the reason for the rates failure has been identified.

This will be corrected in the app in the upcoming release. As of now, we do not have any exact timeline of the enhancement, however, we will update you as soon as we have clarity on the same.

Feel free to reach out if you have any further queries in the meantime.

Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive
